title: Produce Q4_K_M GGUF for DeepSeek V4 Flash

history.note: Created. Plan pre-written per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-05-v4-quants-kvcache-design.md. Architect should adopt the plan and proceed directly to plan-review.

Plan v1 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is per spec. plan_path already set; transitioning directly to plan-review per orchestrator override (architect step is administrative).

Round 1 codex plan-review (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only). Verdict REVISE with 4 blocking issues against v1 plan: missing gguf-py V4 prerequisites, wrong GGUFWriter API, wrong KV key names, wrong HF tensor naming.
Round 2 codex plan-review (high reasoning). REVISE again, deeper findings after inspecting cloned safetensors. v1 plan unsalvageable.

Escalated: orchestrator override forbade plan modification but codex review found showstopper defects in v1 plan. Conflict cannot be resolved within orchestrator authority. v1 plan attempted from-scratch design; codex correctly identified that approach was wrong.

Plan rewritten as v2 (cherry-pick antirez's existing V4 converter from antirez/main, mirroring the Task A runtime port playbook). Both gguf-py V4 enums (~235 lines) and convert_hf_to_gguf.py V4 class (~1082 lines) come from antirez's known-good fork — the same fork that produced the IQ2XXS GGUF we've been running. v1's design-from-scratch error class is eliminated. Spec at .claude/agents/v4-port-H-quants.md updated with the v2 plan reference and conflict resolution rule. Ready for re-dispatch.

Plan v2 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is per spec/orchestrator override. Preconditions verified: ~/models/DeepSeek-V4-Flash/ clone complete (46 safetensors, no .lock/.tmp), config.json shows architectures=['DeepseekV4ForCausalLM'] model_type=deepseek_v4, 1.4 TiB free on ~/models volume (>=800 GiB required), antirez remote correctly configured with V4 commits, gguf.MODEL_ARCH.DEEPSEEK4 absent (port needed). Transitioning directly to plan-review.

Cleared in_progress: previous orchestrator backgrounded codex and exited prematurely (codex went orphan PID 25524 PPID=1, killed). Output was not captured — stdout was attached to dead orchestrator's pipe. Re-dispatching with explicit synchronous-codex instruction.

Round 1 codex plan-review of v2 (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only, synchronous tee to /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v2.log). Verdict REVISE with 2 substantive blocking issues, both verified against antirez source: (1) port mechanics would silently delete V3.2/DSA support because antirez's diff is NOT additive — it renames DEEPSEEK32->DEEPSEEK4 and DeepseekV32ForCausalLM->DeepseekV4Model; the 'git checkout antirez/main -- <file>' approach plus the patch-fallback both fail to merge, they replace; (2) f16 conversion path unreachable — antirez's DeepseekV4Model._write_deepseek4_expert_tensors raises NotImplementedError for FP4 routed experts unless --outtype is one of iq2_xxs/iq2_xs/q2_k/tq2_0/tq1_0/q8_0 (verified at antirez/main:convert_hf_to_gguf.py:9532-9539), so 'python3 convert_hf_to_gguf.py ... --outtype f16' will fail at conversion time. Plan needs explicit FP4-aware intermediate strategy (likely q8_0 intermediate) and must verify the q8_0 -> Q4_K_M llama-quantize path is valid for V4.

Escalating to needs-human at review_round=1 (rather than waiting for round 2) for two reasons: (1) Codex caught real, verified plan defects on the human-authored v2 plan — both issues require non-trivial plan revision (FP4 expert handling strategy, additive-vs-replacing port mechanics) that should be reasoned about by the plan author, not auto-approved; (2) the harness this orchestrator runs in does NOT expose an Agent/Task tool for sub-agent dispatch (verified via ToolSearch — only EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree/Monitor/TaskStop/etc. surfaced, no Agent/Task), so the dispatch instruction's required builder-via-Agent step is unreachable from this session even if plan-review eventually APPROVE'd. The previous v1->v2 cycle used the same escalation pattern: codex REVISE, human rewrites the plan offline, re-dispatches. Same path here: human revises plan v2->v3 to address the two issues in errors[], then re-dispatches. Codex log preserved at /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v2.log for reference.

Plan rewritten as v3 addressing both v2 codex findings: (1) replaced wholesale `git checkout antirez/main -- file` with surgical V4-additive port that preserves V3.2 entries (Task 2 Step 4 and Task 3 Step 4 explicitly verify both DEEPSEEK32 and DEEPSEEK4 coexist after each port operation); (2) changed `--outtype f16` to `--outtype q8_0` throughout (V4 FP4 routed experts force a compact output type per antirez's `_write_deepseek4_expert_tensors` constraint). Disk requirement reduced from 800 GiB to 500 GiB. Spec at .claude/agents/v4-port-H-quants.md updated with v3 plan reference and refined conflict resolution rule. Errors[] cleared since v3 plan addresses both. Ready for re-dispatch.

Plan v3 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is per spec/orchestrator override (architect step administrative). Preconditions verified: V3.2 entries present at gguf-py/gguf/constants.py:445/932/2821/3990 and convert_hf_to_gguf.py:9221-9222; DEEPSEEK4 absent (port needed); ~/models/DeepSeek-V4-Flash/ has 46 safetensors + config.json; 1.4 TiB free (>=500 GiB required); antirez remote configured and fetchable. Transitioning directly to plan-review.

Round 1 codex plan-review of v3 (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only/workspace-write, synchronous tee to /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v3.log). Verdict REVISE with 3 substantive blocking issues, all verified against antirez source AND local cloned safetensors: (1) Task 3 only ports imports/helpers/DeepseekV4Model class but omits the CLI/constructor plumbing antirez added — parse_args() additions at antirez:14057-14065 (--deepseek4-expert-outtypes, --deepseek4-max-layers, --deepseek4-expert-workers), ModelBase.__init__ params, ftype_map entries for iq2_xxs/iq2_xs/q2_k, and the model_class(...) call-site threading those args. Task 4 Step 2 calls --deepseek4-max-layers, so smoke-test is unreachable without this. (2) F8_E8M0 dtype support in LazyTorchTensor not explicitly called out — antirez adds TORCH_FLOAT8_E8M0FNU=getattr(torch,'float8_e8m0fnu',None) at line 61, _dtype_str_map entries for F8_E8M0/F8_E8M0FNU at line 13895, and _dtype_byteswap_map support. The cloned V4 safetensors use F8_E8M0 heavily for scales, so without these the loader cannot read the tensors at all. Plan's 'FP8/I8/FP4 dequant infrastructure' wording is too vague to guarantee these are added. (3) Task 2 constants.py shopping list omits MODEL_TENSOR.ATTN_KV, ATTN_OUT_A, ATTN_OUT_B, FFN_GATE_TID2EID (antirez adds these at constants.py:656-740), ExpertGatingFuncType.SQRTSOFTPLUS at antirez line 4129, and the matching score_func=='sqrtsoftplus' branch in TextModel.set_gguf_parameters() (antirez:1198). Verified against local config.json which has scoring_func='sqrtsoftplus' — converter will fail at parameter setup without this branch.

Escalating to needs-human at review_round=1 per dispatch's escalation rule ('On v3 REVISE with substantive findings (not nitpicks), escalate to needs-human after 2 rounds rather than auto-approve. Auto-approving on a known-bad plan wastes builder budget'). All three codex findings are substantive correctness issues with concrete antirez-source evidence and local-config verification, not nits. They require plan-author judgment about (a) where to insert CLI plumbing (parse_args section, ModelBase.__init__ signature contract), (b) F8_E8M0 dtype gating (torch version conditional), (c) whether SQRTSOFTPLUS handling conflicts with existing scoring_func code on feat/v4-port. The orchestrator override forbids the architect from modifying the plan. Same pattern as v1->v2 and v2->v3: codex REVISE, human rewrites the plan offline, re-dispatches. Codex log preserved at /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v3.log for plan-author reference. Errors[] populated with the 3 issues for the v3->v4 plan revision.

Plan rewritten as v4: incorporates v3 codex findings (CLI/argparse plumbing, F8_E8M0 dtype support, sqrtsoftplus branch + missing tensor enums) as explicit Task 3 substeps 3.A-3.I with antirez line references. Task 2 expanded to include MODEL_TENSOR.{ATTN_KV, ATTN_OUT_A, ATTN_OUT_B, FFN_GATE_TID2EID} and ExpertGatingFuncType.SQRTSOFTPLUS. ALSO: lifting the 'do not modify plan' override on the next dispatch — the architect can revise v4 if codex finds further issues, per standard pipeline. Stop forcing escalation at every codex round.

Plan v4 pre-written; adopting docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-05-v4-port-H-quants.md as-is (architect step administrative; substantive plan changes happen on REVISE per relaxed dispatch rules). Transitioning directly to plan-review for codex round 1.

Round 1 codex plan-review of v4 (high reasoning, sandbox=read-only/workspace-write, synchronous tee to /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v4-r1.log). Verdict APPROVE. Codex inspected antirez/main vs feat/v4-port diff for both gguf-py files and convert_hf_to_gguf.py, verified the plan's enumerated touch surface (TORCH_FLOAT8_E8M0FNU constant, DeepseekV4ForCausalLM register decorator, DeepseekV4Model class, V4 KV constants HASH_LAYER_COUNT/HYPER_CONNECTION_*/ATTENTION_INDEXER_*/ATTENTION_COMPRESS_*/ATTENTION_OUTPUT_*, NEXTN_PREDICT_LAYERS already present from earlier work) covers everything antirez actually adds. Tokens used: 37,833. Plan v4 stands as-is for implementation. Setting review_rounds=0 (APPROVE resets) and proceeding to implementing.

Escalating to needs-human despite plan-review APPROVE: this harness does not expose Agent/Task tools, so the dispatch instruction's required builder step (Agent({run_in_background: true, isolation: 'worktree', subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt: '<builder prompt>'})) is unreachable. ToolSearch surfaces only EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree/Monitor/TaskStop/NotebookEdit/WebFetch/WebSearch/Gmail/Calendar/Drive — no Agent or Task tool. Same harness limitation that produced the v2 escalation note. The plan is APPROVE'd, codex log at /tmp/v4-H-plan-review-v4-r1.log preserved (37,833 tokens, ~1422 lines, double APPROVE at end). Parent orchestrator (which exposes Agent dispatch) should pick up at state=implementing — flip state to 'implementing' and dispatch the builder per the relaxed v4 dispatch (architect may also iterate further on plan if builder hits issues, per the lifted 'do not modify plan' override). NOTE for the parent: plan-review verdict is recorded with review_rounds=0 (APPROVE resets), so the standard implementing→code-review→testing pipeline applies cleanly from here.

Codex plan-review v4 APPROVE'd round 1. Orchestrator escalated to needs-human only because its harness lacked Agent tool to dispatch builder — not a plan defect. Parent session has Agent; takes over pipeline directly. State -> implementing; dispatching builder subagent for Tasks 1-9 of plan v4 in worktree feat/v4-port-H-quants off feat/v4-port.

Plan Task 1 complete: preconditions verified (46 safetensors, V4 arch confirmed, V3.2 entries at constants.py:445/932/2851/4020 + class at convert_hf_to_gguf.py:9221, DEEPSEEK4 absent, 1.4 TiB free, antirez remote OK).

Plan Task 2 complete: gguf-py V4 surgical port committed (93d1b94d9). Both DEEPSEEK32 (37 tensors) and DEEPSEEK4 (41 tensors) coexist; all 8 writer helpers added; SOFTMAX_WEIGHT/SQRTSOFTPLUS added to ExpertGatingFuncType.

Plan Task 3 complete (3.A-3.I): DeepseekV4Model class added to convert_hf_to_gguf.py (01a13fa01). All antirez touch surface covered: imports + TORCH_FLOAT8_E8M0FNU; LazyTorchTensor F8_E8M0 dtype maps; TextModel sqrtsoftplus branch; argparse --deepseek4-* flags + outtype choices; ModelBase.__init__ signature; ftype_map iq2_xxs/iq2_xs/q2_k; model_class call site; ~626-line DeepseekV4Model class with FP8/I8/FP4 dequant pipeline. ModelBase.from_model_architecture confirms both DeepseekV32Model and DeepseekV4Model registered.

Plan Task 4 complete: smoke test passed. Required uv venv with torch>=2.7 (for float8_e8m0fnu) and transformers~=4.50 (5.5.1 broke V4 config parsing). 2-layer conversion produced /tmp/v4-smoke.gguf (15.1G); gate-loader.sh PASSED with arch=deepseek4 detected. All 47 tensor patterns from cloned safetensors are handled by DeepseekV4Model._map_tensor_name_deepseek4 + _prepare_deepseek4_scaled_tensors.

errors: HARNESS LIMITATION: this orchestrator session has no Agent/Task tool to dispatch the builder. Re-dispatch from a parent orchestrator that exposes Agent({run_in_background: true, isolation: 'worktree'}). Plan v4 is codex-APPROVE'd; advance state from 'needs-human' to 'implementing' on re-dispatch.
